name: Reusable Development Workflow Configuration
description: Single source of truth for branching, quality gates, testing, docs hygiene, and commit automation. Set projectType and customize the active 'commands' block. Scripts read this file to stay type-appropriate.
_projectTypeGuidance: THE most important field for portability. Scripts (prepare-commit, hooks, etc.) read this + the matching profile so they never blindly run 'eslint' or 'tsc' on a Python project. After picking a type, copy its profile into the top-level 'commands' object and edit the concrete command strings for your repo. Starters: node-fullstack, python, generic.

node-fullstack.checks: npm run format:check && npm run lint && npx tsc --noEmit && cd dashboard && npx tsc --noEmit
node-fullstack.build: npm run build && cd dashboard && npm run build
node-fullstack.notes: Full-stack TypeScript SaaS (Next.js + Node/Fastify backend). All the TS + Vitest + Playwright tooling applies.

python.build: python -m py_compile $(find . -name '*.py' 2>/dev/null | head -30) 2>/dev/null || echo '(no build step or replace with mypy)'
python.docDriftCheck: echo 'Define your doc/schema drift detector here (or empty string for none)'
python.e2e: echo 'No E2E configured (replace with pytest-playwright or similar if you have browser tests)'
python.notes: Pure Python (FastAPI, Django, Flask, CLI tools, etc.). ruff + black + pytest are the expected tools. No npm, no TypeScript.

generic.checks: echo 'Replace with: your lint + typecheck + format equivalent (one command)'
generic.unitTests: echo 'Replace with your test runner (pytest, go test, cargo test, etc.)'
generic.build: echo 'Replace with your build (go build, cargo build, or empty for dynamic languages)'
generic.formatCheck: echo 'Replace with black --check / gofmt / prettier or empty'
generic.docDriftCheck: echo 'Your doc drift or schema drift detector (or empty)'
generic.focusedTestScan: echo 'Grep expression for focused/skip tests in your language, or empty to skip'
generic.notes: Starting point for anything else (Go, Rust, mixed, mobile, etc.). You must fill in the 'commands' block below.

commands._comment: Customized for pixel-agents (root + webview-ui + daemon subprojects + client Rust). Uses existing check-types (covers multiple tsconfigs), format:check, and test split.
commands.checks: npm run format:check && npm run lint && npm run check-types
commands.docDriftCheck: echo 'No automated doc-drift detector yet. Manually ensure CLAUDE.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, TODO.md, and CONTRIBUTING.md are updated on every feature.'
commands.focusedTestScan: grep -r --include="*.test.*" --include="*.spec.*" -E "(\.only\(|\.skip\()" src/ daemon/ webview-ui/ e2e/ --exclude-dir=node_modules 2>/dev/null || true

documentation.driftDetectorScript: echo 'No automated doc-drift detector configured. Follow the internal discipline in CLAUDE.md (update docs on every feature).'
documentation.notes: pixel-agents: VS Code extension + React webview + Node daemon + Rust client. Already has husky + prepare script + strong internal workflow (slash commands). This config drives prepare-commit and hooks.

notes: Edit projectType, copy the matching profile into the 'commands' section, customize the command strings for your actual tools, and the portable scripts will adapt automatically. This is how the same governance travels cleanly to Python, Go, or any other project without forcing Node-only rituals.
